mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with support for various hardware configurations. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware capabilities and potential complexity in setup for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.16.0

```

> This command sets up the repository for the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to function correctly within the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.16.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.16.0

```

> This installs vLLM with the specified version in a new namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example vLLM instance to start serving LLMs.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This creates a simple vLLM instance for the GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm example-vllm -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}'

```

> You should see 'True' if the instance is ready.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the vLLM installation and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This process involves draining the pods, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.16.0

kubectl get vllm example-vllm -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}'

```

> Replace <pod-name> with the name of your vLLM pod.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM instance is still ready after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the repository is not added.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' and ensure the vLLM repository is added correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CustomResourceDefinition already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD for vLLM is already installed.

**Fix:**
Check if the CRD is already present using 'kubectl get crd' and skip the CRD installation step.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM instance may be misconfigured or the model may not be available.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details on the error.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM instance.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests in the vLLM CR.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see 'True' when running 'kubectl get vllm example-vllm -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}'', indicating that the vLLM instance is ready to serve requests.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
